data.title: 5E Advanced Materials Inc expected to post a loss of 66 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• 5E Advanced Materials Inc is expected to show no change in quarterly revenue when it reports results on September 29 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for 5E Advanced Materials Inc is for a loss of 66 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for 5E Advanced Materials Inc is $9.75, about 62.7% above its last closing price of $3.64

data.title: Wall Street advances ahead of payrolls week
data.description:
Key points:
  • All major U.S. stock indexes close higher, but end the week lower
  • Utilities up most among S&P sectors; Cons staples sole percentage loser
  • Dollar off; bitcoin ~flat; gold, crude rise
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield rises to ~4.18%

WALL STREET ADVANCES AHEAD OF PAYROLLS WEEK

U.S. stocks ended in the green on Friday in the wake of a largely as-expected PCE report, exiting the week on a cheerful note ahead of a data-crowded week, culminating in Friday's employment report.

Analysts expect that report to show continued softening in the jobs market, which could clear the path for additional interest rate cuts from the Fed.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 299.97 points, or 0.65%, to 46,247.29, the S&P 500 gained 38.98 points, or 0.59%, to 6,643.70 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 99.37 points, or 0.44%, to 22,484.07.

All three major indexes finished the week lower, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq snapping three-week winning streaks.

Consumer discretionary , utilities , homebuilders (.SPCOMHOME) and housing stocks were the clear outperformers on the day.

Investors appeared to shrug off President Trump's latest round of tariffs, this one aimed at pharmaceuticals, trucks and furniture.

This week's economic data was varied but generally robust, casting doubt on the likelihood of more than one additional rate cut from the Federal Reserve this year.

But while many policymakers have called for a cautioned approach to interest rate reduction, on Friday Fed Vice Chair Michelle Bowman said recent labor market data suggests it's time "to act decisively and proactively to address decreasing labor market dynamism and emerging signs of fragility."

The Commerce Department's crucial PCE report for August showed inflation behaving as expected while personal income and consumer spending surprised to the upside. But disposable income growth fell short, suggesting that rising costs were at least partially responsible for the stronger-than-expected spending increase, and together with the falling saving rate, hinted that consumers are being squeezed, a notion echoed by the University of Michigan's final take on September consumer sentiment.

Next week, the economic docket is fairly crowded, with a slew of labor market data demanding the most attention. The JOLTS report on jobs market churn is expected to show a downtick in job openings, while private payrolls are seen growing by a scant 50,000 in ADP's National Employment index.

On Thursday, Challenger layoffs and initial jobless claims will set the stage for the September employment report due on Friday, which is expected to align with ADP; economists project the U.S. economy grew by 50,000 jobs in September, with average hourly earnings growth and the unemployment rate holding firm at 0.3% and 3.7%, respectively.

Outside of the labor market, pending home sales, home price growth, consumer confidence, PMI, and new factory orders round out the program.

Next week's earnings landscape is somewhat sparse, although results from Carnival and Nike are on deck.

Here's your closing snapshot:

(Stephen Culp)

*****

data.title: Drone maker DJI loses lawsuit to exit Pentagon's list of firms with Chinese military ties
data.description:
By Mike Scarcella

A U.S. judge on Friday rejected a bid by China-based DJI, the world's largest drone maker, to be removed from the U.S. Defense Department’s list of companies allegedly working with Beijing’s military.

In his ruling, U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman in Washington, D.C., said the Defense Department had substantial evidence supporting its finding that DJI, which sells more than half of all U.S. commercial drones, contributes to the “Chinese defense industrial base.”

DJI had urged the court to order its removal from the Pentagon list designating it as a Chinese military company, saying it "is neither owned nor controlled by the Chinese military."

The judge rejected some of the government's other justifications for listing DJI.

DJI in a statement on Friday said it was disappointed that the judge upheld the listing and was evaluating its legal options. "This decision was based on a single rationale that applies to many companies that have never been listed," DJI said.

The Defense Department declined to comment.

Trump has ordered the Defense Department to rename itself the Department of War, a change that will require action by Congress.

Placement on the Pentagon list can prevent a company from accessing certain U.S. contracts, grants and other programs, Friedman said.

U.S. companies face increased national security risks if they do business with an entity that the Defense Department has accused of working with the Chinese military.

In its lawsuit, DJI said the Defense Department’s addition of the company on the list was "unlawful and misguided." It said it has "lost business deals, been stigmatized as a national security threat, and been banned from contracting with multiple federal government agencies."

The Justice Department in a court filing told Friedman that the U.S. “has long expressed significant concerns about the national security threat posed by the relationship between Chinese technology companies and the Chinese state.”

Friedman in July ruled for the government in a dispute involving China-based lidar manufacturer Hesai Group, which also sued over the U.S. decision to add it to a list of companies allegedly working with Beijing’s military. Hesai has appealed Friedman’s order.

data.title: SC Jobs Economic Authority Offering $113M in Senior Living Revenue Bonds
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

The South Carolina Jobs Economic Authority is offering $113 million in senior living revenue bonds, with proceeds from the offering slated to finance acquisition, renovation and construction of three senior living facilities in the state.

Proceeds from the offering will be loaned to Connexion Communities Simpsonville, Connexion Communities Travelers Rest, and Connexion Communities Greenville, three South Carolina liability companies. The companies will use the funds to acquire, build and equip a 174-unit senior living care facility, as well as a pair of 66-unit senior living communities.

The bonds will be backed by loan payments, as well as by a debt service reserve fund, according to documents on MuniOS.

The offering will consist of $112.5 million of Series 2025 A-1 senior living revenue bonds, which will be sold in three tranches maturing on Oct. 1, 2035, Oct. 1, 2045 and Oct. 1, 2060. Interest from these bonds is exempt from federal and South Carolina state taxes. The offering also contains $855,000 of federally taxable Series 2025 A-2 bonds, which haven't yet received a maturity date. Interest and yield on the offerings hasn't been set.

The bonds are unrated, and no ratings have been requested.

The South Carolina Jobs Economic Authority acts as a statewide conduit issuer of special obligation revenue bonds and assists borrowers in accessing financial markets and capital, it said.

DA Davidson is listed as the offering's manager.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1554 ET - Canada's reputation that the country can't build big things is unfortunately well earned, says the country's former chief central banker, Stephen Poloz. Prime Minister Mark Carney — also a former Bank of Canada governor — is trying to reverse that image by streamling regulations to help get new trade corridors built. During a quarterly webinar for law firm Osler Hoskin & Harcourt, Poloz recalls his time at state-owned lender Export Development Canada in the late 2000s. EDC was approached in 2008 for financing to build roads and infrastructure to access a massive deposit of critical minerals in Ontario. Debate over building those roads to access the mineral-rich Ring of Fire deposit continues to this day. "This is how we earn a reputation for not being able to get anything done," Poloz says. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulviera)

1530 ET - In its Cold Storage report published Friday afternoon, the USDA says that frozen supplies of pork have declined. Total frozen pork supplies were down 3% through August versus the prior month. Pork supplies are down 13% from this timelast year — with stocks of pork bellies down 25% from the previous month and 8% from the prior year. Frozen beef supplies were less moved, with frozen beef down 1% from the prior month but up 2% versus last year. Live cattle futures finish flat at $2.35025 a pound, while lean hog futures pushed up 2.7% to 91.3 cents a pound. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1523 ET - U.S. natural gas futures settle mixed with the October contract expiring and the front month shifting to November, with about a month left to the April-October storage injection season. "We still believe that utilities and other users are looking ahead to a possible cold winter that could combine with a long-awaited upswing in export activity in prompting some appreciably higher prices by the end of this year," Ritterbusch says in a note. Gas for October delivery goes off the board at $2.835/mmBtu, off 2.4%, and the November contract rises 0.3% to $3.206/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1517 ET - Crude futures close the week higher, spurred by continuing Ukrainian strikes against Russian oil infrastructure and the prospect of additional EU and U.S. sanctions that raise concerns of supply constraint. WTI settles up 1.1% at $65.72 a barrel, a 5.3% weekly gain and highest close since early August. Executives polled Sept. 10-18 by the Dallas Fed lowered their average year-end WTI price expectation to $63.06 a barrel from $68.18 in the 2Q survey. Responses ranged from $50 to $80 a barrel, with 43% seeing the year-end price between $60 and $64.99, and 33% between $65 and $69.99. Brent settles up 1% at $70.13 a barrel, up 5.2% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1503 ET - Canada's main stock market in Toronto has enjoyed a stellar year, with year-to-date gains now exceeding 20% after three quarters. National Bank Financial says Toronto stocks have benefited from a rush into gold, as traders deal with persistent inflation uncertainty fueled by the Trump administration's tariffs and USD depreciation. Gold stocks have surged, and NBF calculates that gold stocks account for 11% of the market capitalization of Toronto-listed equities, or the highest share since an 11.5% peak in 2012. The historical average is 4.7% based on records dating back to the mid-1970s. NBF says it expects the share of market capitalization of gold stocks to surpass the 2012 peak as gold remains attractive as an alternative asset. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1436 ET - Front-month silver futures settled up 3.4% to $46.221 a troy ounce. The white metal closes out the week with an 8.7% gain, the most it's risen in a week since October 2011. This brings futures close to a high of over $47/oz last seen in 2011, with the all-time high of $48.70/oz set in January 1980 not far off either. Silver has risen for six consecutive weeks. Gold also had a strong showing, with the front-month contract closing up 1% to $3,775.30/oz. That's 2.8% that gold has appreciated this week, and six straight weeks that gold futures have closed out the week on a positive note. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1354 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. increased by six to 424 in a fifth straight weekly rise, and was 60 below the year-ago level, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. In the Dallas Fed's 3Q survey of energy companies, 42% of exploration and production executives said they have slightly delayed investment decisions because of oil price uncertainty or cost of production, and 36% said they have significantly delayed decisions. On regulatory changes made since January, 57% said they had reduced break-even costs for new wells by less than $1 a barrel, and 25% estimated reductions between $1 and $1.99 a barrel. Rigs directed at natural gas this week slipped by one to 117, or 18 more than a year ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1312 ET - CBOT soybeans are up 0.1% as traders mull over what China's continued absence in buying U.S. grain exports mean for overall U.S. soybean export demand. "Soybeans remain in limbo as the market reconciles little export interest will likely remain for a few more weeks," says Rich Nelson of Allendale Inc., adding that Chinese demand could again return by December or January. Soybeans have been under pressure throughout September, shedding roughly 4% in that time. Most-active corn futures are down 0.7% in afternoon trade, while wheat falls 1.4%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1245 ET - Winter wheat is essentially fully harvested in the U.S. Plains, which means that new grain is arriving at grain elevators and pressuring CBOT prices. That's why wheat futures are leading the complex lower in afternoon trading. "Not a lot to impact the US wheat crop right now as it starts to head into dormancy in a few weeks and current weather is quite favorable," says Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting in a note. Setzer adds that grains are generally quiet Friday, ahead of next week's US Grains Stocks report and the end of the fiscal third quarter. CBOT wheat is down 1.4%, corn is 1% lower, and soybeans are up 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1138 ET - A joint task force partnering the USDA and DOJ to investigate the high costs for farmer inputs like fertilizer and seeds is getting praise from groups like the National Farmers Union. "When just a handful of companies control the markets for seed, fertilizer and other farm supplies, they can raise prices at will," says the NFU in a note. "This leaves farmers squeezed between skyrocketing costs and low prices for their products." CBOT grain futures are mostly below the levels needed for farmers to turn a profit on their acres, and these farmers need both improved crop prices and lower input costs in order for these acres to turn positive. CBOT grains are mixed, with corn down 0.8%, soybeans up 0.1%, and wheat down 1.2%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1035 ET - Silver futures are pushing towards highs last seen in April 2011, according to data from FactSet. The most-active contract is now up 1.8% to $45.91 a troy ounce. While gold has dominated headlines, analysts are also looking at strong gains elsewhere. "It has been silver and platinum that have delivered the most eye-catching returns," says Ole Hansen of Saxo Bank in a note. Industrial demand has been a factor underpinning strength in silver, he adds, along with investors seeking the metal as an alternative to gold. "The metal's high-beta nature has amplified inflows from investors seeking leverage to gold's record-breaking run," says Hansen. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1018 ET - U.S. natural gas futures are lower heading into the expiration of the October contract following gains the previous three session. The market is seeing little support from the weather with a mild start to October likely to delay early-season heating demand. "Even as LNG begins to rise seasonally, the near-term fundamental outlook for natural gas is bearish as storage fills rapidly and weather remains mild," Eli Rubin of EBW Analytics says in a note. The October contract is off 2.8% at $2.823/mmBtu. Gas for November delivery is down 1.8% at $3.138/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Blackstone Mortgage Trust Announces Third-Quarter 2025 Earnings Release and Conference Call
data.description:
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--September 26, 2025--

Blackstone Mortgage Trust, Inc. (the "Company") today announced that it will publish its third-quarter 2025 earnings presentation on its website at www.bxmt.com and file its Form 10-Q pre-market on Wednesday, October 29, 2025. The Company will also host a conference call the same day at 9:00 a.m. ET to review results.

To register for the webcast, please use the following link: https://event.webcasts.com/starthere.jsp?ei=1735594&tp_key=ec7b6b8798

For those unable to listen to the live broadcast, there will be a webcast replay on the Company's website at www.bxmt.com beginning approximately two hours after the event.

About Blackstone Mortgage Trust

Blackstone Mortgage Trust is a real estate finance company that originates, acquires and manages senior loans and other debt or credit-oriented investments collateralized by or relating to commercial real estate in North America, Europe, and Australia. Our investment objective is to preserve and protect shareholder capital while producing attractive risk-adjusted returns primarily through dividends generated from current income. Our portfolio is composed primarily of loans secured by high-quality, institutional assets in major markets, sponsored by experienced, well-capitalized real estate investment owners and operators. These loans are financed in a variety of ways, depending on our view of the most prudent strategy available for each of our investments. We are externally managed by BXMT Advisors L.L.C., a subsidiary of Blackstone. Further information is available at www.bxmt.com.

About Blackstone

Blackstone is the world's largest alternative asset manager. Blackstone seeks to deliver compelling returns for institutional and individual investors by strengthening the companies in which the firm invests. Blackstone's nearly $1.2 trillion in assets under management include global investment strategies focused on real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, secondaries and hedge funds. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250926391086/en/

CONTACT: Investor Relations

Blackstone

BlackstoneShareholderRelations@Blackstone.com

data.title: Candelaria Provides Update on MCTO
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, B.C.– TheNewswire - September 26, 2025 - Candelaria Mining Corp.(TSX-V: CAND.H, OTC PINK: CDELF) (“Candelaria” or the “Company”), is providing an update to its previously disclosed managementcease trade order ("MCTO"), announced on August 29, 2025, inrespect of the audited annual financial statements and correspondingmanagement's discussion and analysis for the year ended April 30,2025, including the CEO and CFO certifications (collectively, the"Annual Financial Filings") that were not filed by therequired filing deadline of August 28, 2025 (the "FilingDeadline").

As previously disclosed, the AnnualFinancial Filings were not filed by the Filing Deadline due to thefact that the Company’s auditors require additional time to completetheir audit and as a result, challenges with updating the Company’saccounts following the completion of the disposition of certainMexican subsidiaries by the Company.

The Company is working expeditiouslyon the steps required to complete the Annual Financial Filings andexpects to be able to file the Annual Financial Filings on or beforeOctober 3, 2025. The Company will provide updates as furtherinformation relating to the Annual Financial Filings becomesavailable.

Until the Annual Financial Filingsare filed, the MCTO will be in effect. Additionally, the Company willissue bi-weekly default status reports in accordance with NationalPolicy 12-203 - Management Cease Trade Orders. The Company intends tosatisfy the provisions of the Alternative Intonation Guidelines duringthe period it remains in default of the filing requirements. TheCompany confirms that there have been no material businessdevelopments or other material information relating to its affairs asof the date of this news release that has not been generallydisclosed.

On Behalf ofCandelaria

Héctor Félix González Ramirez 

CEO and Director

For further information, pleasecontact:  

Omar García Abrego

CFO

+1 (604) 369-2928 | info@candelariamining.com

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Forward lookingstatements are necessarily based upon a number of estimates andassumptions that, while considered reasonable, are subject to knownand unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors which may causethe actual results and future events to differ materially from thoseexpressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. data.title: ConnectM Tech Solutions Files 8K - Changes To Articles >CNTM
data.description:
ConnectM Technology Solutions Inc. (CNTM) filed a Form 8K - Changes to Articles of Incorporation or Bylaws; Change in Fiscal Year - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on September 26, 2025.

On September 25, 2025, ConnectM Technology Solutions, Inc. (the "Company") filed a Certificate of Amendment (the "Amendment") to the Company's Second Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation with the Delaware Secretary of State. The Amendment had the effect of increasing the total number of authorized shares of the Company's common stock, $0.0001 par value per share, from 100,000,000 to 250,000,000. The Amendment had no effect on the number of authorized shares of preferred stock. Accordingly, following the filing of the Amendment, effective September 25, 2025, the Company's authorized capital stock consisted of 260,000,000 shares, representing (i) 250,000,000 shares of common stock, and (ii) 10,000,000 shares of preferred stock.

The Amendment was approved by the Company's Board of Directors on August 13, 2025, and by the Company's stockholders on September 24, 2025.

The foregoing description of the Amendment is subject to and qualified in its entirety by reference to the full text of the Amendment, a copy of which is attached hereto at Exhibit 3.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and is incorporated by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895249/000110465925093847/tm2527060d1_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1895249/000110465925093847/0001104659-25-093847-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Capital One Financial Corporation to Webcast Conference Call on Third Quarter 2025 Earnings
data.description:
MCLEAN, Va.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--September 26, 2025--

On Tuesday, October 21, 2025, at approximately 4:05 p.m. Eastern Time, Capital One Financial Corporation will release its third quarter 2025 earnings results. Additionally, the company will host a conference call at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time to review financial and operating performance for the quarter ending September 30, 2025.

The call will be webcast live and the earnings release will be available on the company's homepage at www.capitalone.com. A replay of the webcast will be available 24 hours a day, beginning two hours after the conference call, until 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on November 4, 2025, through the company's homepage.

About Capital One

Capital One Financial Corporation (www.capitalone.com) is a financial holding company which, along with its subsidiaries, had $468.1 billion in deposits and $659.0 billion in total assets as of June 30, 2025. Headquartered in McLean, Virginia, Capital One offers a broad spectrum of financial products and services to consumers, small businesses and commercial clients through a variety of channels. Capital One, N.A. has branches and Cafés located primarily in New York, Louisiana, Texas, Maryland, Virginia and the District of Columbia. A Fortune 500 company, Capital One trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "COF" and is included in the S&P 100 index.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250926363470/en/

CONTACT: Media Release

Sie Soheili

Sie.Soheili@capitalone.com

data.title: ALLIANCEBERNSTEIN NATIONAL MUNICIPAL INCOME FUND, INC. REPORTS THIRD QUARTER EARNINGS
data.description:
NEW YORK, Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — AllianceBernstein National Municipal Income Fund, Inc. , a registered closed--end investment company, today announced earnings for the Fund's third fiscal quarter ended July 31, 2025.

Total net assets of the Fund* on July 31, 2025 were $322,750,581 as compared with $337,926,439 on April 30, 2025, and $363,570,286 on July 31, 2024. On July 31, 2025, the net asset value per share of common stock was $11.23 based on 28,744,936 shares of common stock outstanding.

data.title: MOVING iMAGE TECHNOLOGIES INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Moving iMage Technologies Inc., a company specializing in providing technology, products, and services to movie theater operators and sports and entertainment venues, has released its annual 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive market.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $18.147 million, a decrease of 9.9% from the prior year, primarily due to the protracted SAG/AFTRA strike.
  • Gross Profit: $4.573 million, a decrease of 2.3% from the prior year, with gross margin increasing to 25.2% from 23.3% due to product mix changes.
  • Net Loss: $(0.948) million, an improvement of $0.424 million from the prior year, largely due to staff reductions related to headcount reduction in 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Corporate Overview: Moving iMage Technologies, Inc. was incorporated in June 2020 and has evolved through acquisitions and restructuring, including the acquisition of Caddy Products in 2019. The company provides technology, products, and services to movie theater operators and sports and entertainment venues.
  • Product and Service Offerings: The company offers project management services, proprietary products, and resells third-party technologies. Key offerings include ADA-compliant accessibility products, a SaaS platform for theater management, and a translator service for multi-language movie viewing.
  • Industry Trends: The movie exhibition industry is recovering post-pandemic, with a focus on enhancing the movie-going experience through new platforms and product offerings. International markets are increasingly important, contributing significantly to global box office revenues.
  • Technological Advancements: The company is involved in the deployment of laser projectors, which offer significant advantages over traditional lamp-based systems. They are also pioneering Direct View LED screens, which are seen as disruptive to traditional projection methods.
  • New Product Launches: Recent product introductions include a theater management SaaS platform, a translator service for multi-language viewing, and a mobile cart for eSports and gaming in theaters.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced challenges due to the COVID-19 pandemic and the SAG/AFTRA strikes, impacting movie production and releases. However, theaters have largely reopened, and the industry is gradually recovering.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to invest selectively in growth, focusing on expanding its customer base and enhancing its product offerings. It aims to maintain gross margins by introducing new high-margin products and managing inventory effectively.
  • Sales and Marketing Strategy: The company markets directly to theater exhibitors and through value-added resellers, emphasizing its broad range of products and services. It maintains strong relationships with major theater owners both domestically and internationally.
  • Manufacturing and Assembly: The company operates a 21,000 square foot facility in Southern California for manufacturing and assembly, supported by a global network of service partners and OEM manufacturers.
  • Regulatory Environment: The company is subject to various regulations affecting its operations, including environmental, safety, and health requirements. Compliance with these regulations is critical to its business operations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is focusing on expanding its operations selectively, particularly in sales and support, to support new product initiatives and budget goals. It aims to add new customers and expand sales to existing customers by investing in its field sales force and targeting large customer organizations. The company is also promoting its brand and offering additional products to achieve brand recognition and introduce new high-quality products and services.
  • Capital Management: The company has primarily met its working capital and capital resource needs from operating cash flows and financing activities. It has not declared or paid cash dividends, intending to retain future earnings to finance business growth. The company did not engage in any stock buyback program during the fiscal year 2025, and there were no unregistered sales of equity securities. The cash balance increased to $5.715 million as of June 30, 2025, from $5.278 million in the previous year.
  • Future Outlook: The company believes it has sufficient cash to sustain operations and plans to continue investing in growth initiatives. It expects operating expenses to decrease in the foreseeable future to meet revenue and cost control objectives. The company anticipates that a substantial portion of future sales will come from existing customers as it offers new products and services through existing sales channels. It also plans to increase marketing expenditures selectively to maintain brand awareness and introduce new product lines.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: General political, social, and economic conditions can adversely affect the business, especially if there is a downturn in the cinema market. The company is also exposed to foreign currency rate fluctuations and weak economic and political conditions in countries where it sells products.
  • Operational Risks: The company is dependent on significant customers who could cease purchasing products at any time. Supply chain disruptions and the inability to introduce new products timely could adversely affect operations. The company also faces risks related to maintaining and developing sales channels and accurately forecasting consumer demand.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to complex laws and regulations, including environmental, safety, and health requirements, which could be onerous and expensive to comply with.
  • Emerging Risks: The company is exploring new business initiatives, such as the MiTranslator system and Direct View LED screens, which may not be accepted by the market. The company also faces risks related to cybersecurity and data breaches, which could disrupt operations and damage customer relationships.
  • Management Challenges: Management has identified several challenges, including the need to attract and retain highly skilled personnel to manage growth effectively. The company is also focused on maintaining its brand and reputation, which could be harmed by negative publicity or failure to provide high-quality customer support. The company is also exploring strategic opportunities, including acquisitions, which could involve risks such as problems assimilating purchased operations and adverse effects on existing business relationships.
  • Market Risk Exposure: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could adversely affect financial results. The company also faces risks related to changes in the industries in which it operates and variations in operating performance compared to competitors.

SEC Filing: MOVING iMAGE TECHNOLOGIES INC. [ MITQ ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: Lite Strategy, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Lite Strategy, Inc., formerly known as MEI Pharma, Inc., has released its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025. The report details the company's significant strategic pivot from pharmaceutical development to a focus on digital assets, particularly through the implementation of a Litecoin Treasury Strategy. This shift marks a transformative period for the company, accompanied by notable financial and operational changes.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $0 million. The company recognized no revenue during the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025, due to all deferred revenue associated with the KKC Commercialization Agreement having been recognized in fiscal year 2024.
  • Net (Loss) Income: $(15.9) million. The net loss for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025, was primarily due to the absence of revenue and ongoing operating expenses.
  • Net (Loss) Income Per Share - Basic and Diluted: $(2.39). Reflects the net loss per share for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025, based on the weighted-average shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Company Rebranding: On September 10, 2025, MEI Pharma, Inc. changed its name to Lite Strategy, Inc. and its ticker symbol to LITS, marking a significant rebranding effort.
  • Strategic Alternatives Evaluation: In July 2024, the company began evaluating strategic alternatives, including potential transactions and an orderly wind down of operations, leading to a reduction in workforce and a shift in strategic direction.
  • Discontinuation of Voruciclib Development: The clinical development of voruciclib was discontinued as part of the strategic alternatives evaluation, although certain nonclinical activities continue.
  • Leadership Changes: Key leadership changes included the stepping down of the President and CEO, David M. Urso, and the Chief Medical Officer, Richard Ghalie, M.D., with Justin J. File appointed as Acting CEO.
  • Litecoin Treasury Strategy: In August 2025, the company implemented a Litecoin Treasury Strategy, focusing on digital asset infrastructure and long-term capital innovation, including potential Litecoin mining activities.
  • PIPE Financing: In July 2025, the company closed a $100 million Private Investment in Private Equity (PIPE), issuing shares and pre-funded warrants to support its strategic initiatives.
  • Termination of Cooperation Agreement: The cooperation agreement with Anson Funds Management LP and Cable Car Capital LLC was terminated in July 2025, following the closing of the PIPE offering.
  • Merger Termination: The proposed merger with Infinity Pharmaceuticals, Inc. was terminated in July 2023 after failing to receive necessary stockholder approval.
  • Zandelisib Development Discontinuation: The global development of zandelisib was discontinued following regulatory guidance, with all related clinical studies outside Japan closed.
  • Future Outlook: The company is committed to long-term innovation in capital structure and financial technology, with plans to continue assessing pre-clinical activities and potential investigational research and development.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Shift: Lite Strategy, Inc. implemented a significant strategic shift by transitioning from a pharmaceutical focus to a digital asset strategy, specifically through the acquisition of Litecoin tokens as part of their Litecoin Treasury Strategy. This move reflects a commitment to long-term innovation in capital structure and financial technology.
  • Capital Management: The company raised $100 million through a Private Investment in Public Equity (PIPE) offering, issuing common stock and pre-funded warrants. This capital was used to support the Litecoin Treasury Strategy. Additionally, the company paid a cash dividend of $1.75 per share, totaling $11.7 million, as part of a cooperation agreement with Anson Funds Management LP and Cable Car Capital LLC. The company also terminated its lease agreement, resulting in a significant reduction in liabilities.
  • Future Outlook: Lite Strategy, Inc. plans to continue its focus on digital asset management and financial technology innovation, with potential expansion into Litecoin mining activities. The company is also assessing the recommencement of pre-clinical development for its drug candidates, indicating a dual focus on digital assets and pharmaceutical research. The current cash balance is expected to fund operations for at least the next 12 months, with future capital requirements dependent on the scope of the Litecoin Treasury Strategy and other strategic initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Regulatory Risks: The company faces significant risks related to its investment in Litecoin, a highly volatile asset. The price of Litecoin has fluctuated significantly, and future price declines could negatively impact the company's financial condition. Additionally, the introduction of central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) could reduce the demand for cryptocurrencies like Litecoin, further impacting the company's strategy.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Changes in the classification of Litecoin as a security could subject the company to additional regulatory requirements under the Investment Company Act of 1940, leading to increased costs and operational changes.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The company is exposed to cybersecurity risks, particularly concerning the security of its Litecoin holdings. A breach or cyberattack could result in significant financial losses and reputational damage.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to monitor regulatory developments closely and adjust its Litecoin holdings to avoid falling under the definition of an 'investment company.' The company plans to reduce its Litecoin holdings if necessary and may seek to acquire additional non-investment securities to maintain compliance.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks related to the volatility of Litecoin prices and potential regulatory changes. These factors could adversely affect the company's financial results and liquidity. The company also faces risks from potential economic downturns and disruptions in capital markets, which could limit its ability to raise additional capital or implement its Litecoin Treasury Strategy effectively.

SEC Filing: Lite Strategy, Inc. [ LITS ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: U.S. Stocks Rise To Close Choppy Week — Market Talk
data.description:
1609 ET - U.S. stocks climb as traders weigh the latest new tariffs on drugs, trucks and furniture. Shares of U.S. drug makers rise as many have committed to construction in America in recent months, and many already have some production there. The rebound helps major indexes snap a three-day losing streak, ending lower for the week. DJIA gains 300 points, or 0.7%, to 46247, the S&P 500 rises 0.6% to 6643 and the Nasdaq adds 0.4% to 22484. The University of Michigan's consumer survey found views on the economy dropped this month, while the Fed's preferred inflation measure stayed above target. September payrolls data are due next Friday. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1554 ET - Canada's reputation that the country can't build big things is unfortunately well earned, says the country's former chief central banker, Stephen Poloz. Prime Minister Mark Carney — also a former Bank of Canada governor — is trying to reverse that image by streamling regulations to help get new trade corridors built. During a quarterly webinar for law firm Osler Hoskin & Harcourt, Poloz recalls his time at state-owned lender Export Development Canada in the late 2000s. EDC was approached in 2008 for financing to build roads and infrastructure to access a massive deposit of critical minerals in Ontario. Debate over building those roads to access the mineral-rich Ring of Fire deposit continues to this day. "This is how we earn a reputation for not being able to get anything done," Poloz says. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulviera)

1551 ET - Treasury yields rise for the week, despite today's mixed performance. Next week brings September employment data seen as fundamental to determine the Fed's next steps. BNP Paribas' Andrew Husby forecasts the addition of 60,000 jobs and unemployment steady at 4.3%, keeping the Fed on for consecutive cuts this year. Husby says the data release could be delayed by a potential government-shutdown. In that case, "the ADP employment report [due Wednesday] could receive more attention than usual," he says. The 10-year yield rises 0.047 percentage point this week, to 4.185%. The two-year is up 0.064 p.p. to 3.646%. The WSJ Dollar Index rises 0.6% for the week. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1515 ET - The increased use of artificial intelligence by companies and organization is likely a factor in a recent string of "pretty bad" jobs data in Canada, says Stephen Poloz, the former Bank of Canada governor. During a quarterly presentation hosted by his employer, Toronto law firm Osler Hoskin & Harcourt, Poloz says the current period of tariff-fueled elevated uncertainty has companies jumping on opportunities to cut costs. Among them is automation, or the deployment of AI. "I think we're starting to see the evidence of the fourth industrial revolution starting to cut into that front line of new job creation," Poloz says. Bearing the brunt are Canada's youth, Poloz adds, noting that the unemployment rate for the 15-to-24 cohort sits at 14.5%, or well above the 7.1% national jobless rate. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1503 ET - Canada's main stock market in Toronto has enjoyed a stellar year, with year-to-date gains now exceeding 20% after three quarters. National Bank Financial says Toronto stocks have benefited from a rush into gold, as traders deal with persistent inflation uncertainty fueled by the Trump administration's tariffs and USD depreciation. Gold stocks have surged, and NBF calculates that gold stocks account for 11% of the market capitalization of Toronto-listed equities, or the highest share since an 11.5% peak in 2012. The historical average is 4.7% based on records dating back to the mid-1970s. NBF says it expects the share of market capitalization of gold stocks to surpass the 2012 peak as gold remains attractive as an alternative asset. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

1450 ET - Costco Wholesale could gain more sales growth from its extended hours for executive members, Bank of America analysts Robert Ohmes and Madeline Cech say. The retailer gained 1% in weekly U.S. sales from the extended hours during the F4Q after implementing them in June. The analysts expect that boost to continue, along with benefits from Costco's membership fee increase and planned warehouse openings in fiscal 2026. Costco paid membership and executive membership penetration is still growing, which, for Ohmes and Cech, offsets any concern about recent declines in overall membership renewal rates. Costco is off 2.7%. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1428 ET - The tariff landscape for the furniture industry is in a fluid situation after President Trump promised to impose a 30% levy on furniture imports, Raymond James analyst Bobby Griffin tells WSJ in an interview. Griffin says investors were already expecting some degree of tariffs after Trump in August said furniture imports would be investigated. But it is still uncertain whether the new rates will be stacked upon existing reciprocal tariff rates with particular countries or will replace those rates. That could make a big difference with many furniture companies already dealing with high tariffs for their imports from Vietnam and China. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1337 ET - Ulta is in a better spot to face competition as industry dynamics improve while the company makes progress on its internal execution, UBS analysts say in a note. Demand seems to be improving across the beauty industry while promotions appear rational, setting up a supportive backdrop for Ulta to see a high return on investment as it invests further in its product assortment and guest experience, the analysts say. The company is also poised to gain market share as drug and department stores continue to close locations, they say. Such dynamics should further support the stock's outperformance, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1331 ET - Consolidation in the homebuilding industry has helped make it better able to keep building through downturns, according to John Burns Research & Consulting analyst Jody Kahn. Big builders with significant capital compose 61% of the market in 2024, up from 24% in 2003, a portion that will likely keep growing. These builders can better secure low-cost financing, compete for land and labor and negotiate for lower prices, making the industry less sensitive to demand cycles. "Smaller local builders often struggle during periods of low demand, while larger, capital-backed homebuilders are better positioned to keep building, capitalize on growth cycles, and potentially stabilize the new home sector," Kahn says. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1319 ET - Speciality beauty and mass retailers continue to beat out drug and department stores in the beauty category, according to UBS in a note. The analysts say they found a sharp discrepancy in sales performance between players like Ulta, Sephora, Walmart and Amazon versus the likes of CVS, RiteAid, Walgreens, Macy's, Kohl's and Nordstrom. Those drug and department stores also continue to close stores, shutting down several hundred in the first half of the year, they say. That should allow room for further market share gains from differentiated players like Ulta going forward, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1309 ET - Okta's security product for AI agents likely won't add any material contributions for the company's growth until fiscal 2028 at the earliest, D.A. Davidson analysts Rudy Kessinger and Andres Miranda Lopez say in a note after attending the company's annual user event. The product will be available in fiscal 2027 and aims to discover risky agents and centrally control agent access, among other functions, the analysts say. Several customers said they are interested in learning more about the offering, but the vast majority are not planning any significant AI agent rollouts anytime soon, the analysts say. Okta management indicates the product will not be a big growth opportunity in the near term, but should be significant in the long term, the analysts say.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1259 ET - KB Home may face obstacles in its path back to build-to-order homes, say Zelman & Associates analysts. Right now, there is an abundance of spec inventory on the market and KB Home's competitors are offering aggressive incentives to offload it. That may make it difficult for the company to compete using its build-to-order offerings, especially as it has adopted the strategy of cutting price rather than offering incentives. "It remains to be seen if KBH is able to differentiate itself as a build-to-order builder focusing on transparent pricing rather than an incentive-heavy pricing approach," the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: SOS Limited Reports 2025 Semi-Annual Financial Results
data.description:
QINGDAO, China, Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — SOS Limited ("SOS" or the "Company") today reported its semi-annual financial results for the six-months ended June 30, 2025.

In compliance with the New York Stock Exchange rules, the Form 6-K is available on the Company's website at http://www.sosyun.com/. In addition, all shareholders of the Company may request, free of charge, a hard copy of the Company's complete audited financial statements filed with the SEC. To request a hard copy of the Company's audited financial statements, or for any other inquiry in respect of this press release, please contact the Investor Relations Department of the Company, whose contact information is as follows: ir@sosyun.com.

Interim Financial Results from Operations

Six months ended June 30, 2025 compared to June 30, 2024

Results of operations

Revenue

As of June 30, 2025, SOS focuses on three product and service lines including Commodities Trading, Hosting Service and Others. Commodities Trading contributes 94.9% of the total revenue, 4.3% from Hosting Services and 0.8% from Others, (i.e. legacy data mining which was disposed of in November 2022).

Net revenue was $89.6 million, up 48.1% from $60.5 million period-on-period. An increase in revenue is primarily due to higher sales of natural rubber, one of the key industrial materials, as the Chinese economy has continued to recover over the past six months. The ongoing recovery of the Chinese economy over the past six months has fueled increased demand for rubber products. There was no output of production from cryptocurrency mining business: to optimize the use of our limited infrastructure--specifically, physical space and electrical power--the company has decided to strategically reallocate resources toward our higher-growth segment. Effective immediately, the Company shut down temporary (at least during first half of the year) the internal Bitcoin mining operations (self-mining) to prioritize and expand our third-party mining hosting services, which have demonstrated strong and increasing demand.

              ======  ==========      ======  ==========

Costs of revenue

Operating costs were $90.9 million, up 56.5% period-on-period from $58.1 million in the six months ended June 30, 2024 which is consistent with our revenue increase. Operating costs comprised of depreciation of hardware, electricity power and depreciation from property equipment for cryptocurrency mining as well as costs of goods sold & warehouse rental for commodity trading.

Operating expenses

Selling Expenses

Selling expenses mainly relate to our commodity business and include freight-out expenses, custom clearing agency fee, warehouse rental expense, promotional expense, sales commission and payroll expenses to sales team. Selling expenses increased to $2.3 million from $2.2 million for the same period last year mainly attributable to the higher transportation costs.

General and Administrative Expenses

General and administrative expenses were $9.4 million, 35.0% down period-on-period from $14.5 million in the end of 2024. The decrease was mainly due to the fact that the mining machines have been fully impaired and no depreciation expense is recorded for them in this period.

Operating Loss

GAAP net loss was $14.2 million, compared to a net loss of $10.9 million in the six months ended of 2024, representing an increase of 30.3%. We concluded the period with a gross margin of -1.5%. This result is attributed to a sharp decline in the fair market price of our key products, rubber and petroleum coke. Specifically, we sold off a backlog of low-quality petroleum coke inventory at cost, and falling rubber spot prices caused a loss of over $1.6 million. In the face of slowing low market demand in petroleum code, our strategy has been to prioritize market share over immediate profitability for certain products. We are consciously offering competitive pricing with thin or negative margins to establish a foothold for future business expansion.

Income Tax

The Company paid $3,000 of corporate income tax for the current period as compared to NIL at the end of the six months ended June 30, 2024.

GAAP net loss attributable to ordinary shareholders was $14.2 million, as compared to a net loss of $10.9 million in the six months ended June 30, 2024.

GAAP Basic EPS was $(0. 0139) per share, as compared to $(0.0299) per share in the six months ended June 30, 2024.

data.title: S&P 500 Rises 38.98 Points (0.59%)
Nasdaq Composite Rises 99.37 Points (0.44%)
DJIA Rises 299.97 Points (0.65%)
Costco is among the S&P 500's worst performers today. An analyst still says it's a 'safe place to hide.'
data.description:
By Bill Peters

However, analysts note increasing competition from Sam's Club and BJ's

Costco reported quarterly earnings on Thursday.

Shares of Costco Wholesale Corp. were down 2.5% on Friday, making it one of the S&P 500's worst performers for the day, following quarterly results a day earlier that were marked by weaker-than-expected same-store sales and slipping membership-renewal rates.

Wall Street, however, still considers the membership warehouse retailer a good bet.

"We believe Costco (COST) remains a safe place to hide for many investors looking for consistency and operational excellence against an uncertain consumer backdrop" marked by worries about tariffs and price increases, William Blair analyst Phillip Blee said in a research note on Friday.

Blee added that Costco's specialized team of merchants gave the company an advantage against a hard-to-predict tariff backdrop. And he said demand for discretionary goods - that is, things people don't necessarily need to buy - was still solid.

He said Costco put up a high-single-digit same-store sales gain in nonfood items. That was better than the most recent results for Walmart Inc. (WMT) and its subsidiary Sam's Club, as well as Target Corp. (TGT) and B.J.'s Wholesale Club Holdings Inc. (BJ).

But he noted that Costco was "not the best proxy for overall consumer health," given that its customers tend to have higher incomes. William Blair's most recent survey data indicated that average household income for Costco members was around 27% higher than for U.S. consumers overall.

As U.S. tariffs on imports take hold, analysts have said Costco has the size, flexibility and bargaining power with suppliers to keep prices lower. On Thursday, Costco executives said extra hours for its Executive members, as well as a deal with Instacart, had drawn more shoppers to that top-tier membership class.

However, Costco's stock is only up around 0.1% so far this year. That chart action follows a larger run higher in years past, as value-focused customers flocked to the chain to get a break from inflation.

Roth analyst Bill Kirk, in a note on Friday, said Costco was still the nation's leading warehouse-club chain. But he said the competition could get tougher.

"By leveraging the best private-label program to create compelling economics for shoppers, Costco scores in the top decile of food retailers (according to Yelp!) and also as the top warehouse club concept," he said.

"However, with smaller households and delayed household formation (not new trends), we are less confident warehouse club sub-segment can sustain market share gains," he added. "With increased investment behind competitors, Sam's Club ... and BJ's ..., we expect segment and Costco performance to converge toward conventional food retailers."

Brian Mulberry, a senior portfolio manager at Zacks, said in emailed commentary that margin pressure from higher costs related to imports, logistics and raw materials was still a "key risk" for Costco.

"How well Costco manages its cost structure, inventory, and pricing strategy in the face of inflation and tariffs will be critical going forward," he said.

Other analysts noted the profit pressure on Costco but remained upbeat.

CFRA analyst Arun Sundaram said that Costco's operating margins were below those of most of its big-box rivals. But he said the company was still in the early innings of its rollout of retail media, the business segment that allows brands to advertise in retailers' physical and e-commerce stores. Revenue in that segment, Sundaram said, tends to come with higher profit margins.

Elsewhere, D.A. Davidson analyst Michael Baker, in a note on Thursday, said Costco's "premium" stock valuation "already captures its premium offering."

Costco is still "one of the best retailers in the world," he said. "But with that comes very high expectations."

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Key facts: Evercore ISI Group has lowered its price target for Mondelez International Inc. (MDLZ) from $73.00 to $72.00 per share.
data.description:
• Evercore ISI Group has lowered its price target for Mondelez International Inc. (MDLZ) from $73.00 to $72.00 per share.1
  • U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is urging Mondelez International Inc. to remove synthetic dyes by next year, highlighting voluntary commitments over regulations.2

data.title: Key facts: Eli Lilly's drug Kisunla (donanemab) has gained European Commission approval for early symptomatic Alzheimer's treatment, showing significant benefits in slowing cognitive decline in mild cases.
data.description:
• Eli Lilly's drug Kisunla (donanemab) has gained European Commission approval for early symptomatic Alzheimer's treatment, showing significant benefits in slowing cognitive decline in mild cases.12
  • Eli Lilly (LLY) shares fell 3.1% after halting a mid-stage trial for bimagrumab, aimed at preventing muscle loss in obesity treatment Zepbound, due to 'strategic business reasons'.3
  • Eli Lilly plans a $5 billion investment in a Virginia manufacturing facility, part of a $27 billion expansion that includes four new U.S. plants over the next five years.4

data.title: Key facts: Citigroup raised its price target for Morgan Stanley shares to $155.00 from $130.00.
data.description:
• Citigroup raised its price target for Morgan Stanley shares to $155.00 from $130.00.1
  • Morgan Stanley acted as a global coordinator and lead manager for Abu Dhabi's $3 billion dual tranche bond issuance, comprising a $1 billion 3-year bond and a $2 billion 10-year bond.2
  • Morgan Stanley's chief economist, Ellen Zentner, noted stable inflation, hinting at a potential Federal Reserve rate cut in late October, depending on the upcoming jobs report.3

data.title: Key facts: Chevron announced it expects a financial impact of $200 million to $400 million in the third quarter as a result of its deal with Hess.
data.description:
• Chevron announced it expects a financial impact of $200 million to $400 million in the third quarter as a result of its deal with Hess.1
  • Chevron's stock rose by 2.1% as crude prices increased following Russia's decision to cut fuel exports in response to Ukraine's attacks on its energy infrastructure.2
  • Chevron, a shareholder in the Caspian Pipeline Consortium, faces impacts from halted tanker loading at Novorossiisk following a Ukrainian drone strike, raising global energy security concerns.3
